title: Add automatic DCO signing via Git hooks to simplify contributor workflow
body:
### Feature request description

A clear and concise description of what the feature request is about


### Problem
Contributors frequently encounter DCO (Developer Certificate of Origin) check failures because they forget to add the `Signed-off-by` trailer to their commits. This creates friction in the contribution process and requires:
- Amending commits with `--signoff`
- Force-pushing to update PRs
- Additional CI re-runs


### Proposed Solution
Add a Git hook that automatically signs commits, similar to what other CNCF projects use (e.g., Kubernetes, containerd).

### Implementation
Add two files to the repository:

1. **`.githooks/prepare-commit-msg`** - Hook script that automatically adds DCO signoff:
2. Add to makefile- init-git-hooks


### Benefits

- Reduces friction: Contributors no longer need to remember --signoff or [git commit -s](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code%202.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)
- Fewer CI failures: Automatic DCO signing means fewer failed checks
- Better developer experience: One-time setup with [make init-git-hooks](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code%202.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)
- Standard practice: Matches workflow used by other CNCF projects

### Suggest potential solution

A clear and concise description of what you want to happen.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
